children.text: references to sections 6293, 6294, and 6295 of this title shall be considered as references to sections 6314, 6315, and 6313 of this title, respectively;
references to “this part” shall be treated as referring to part A–1;
the term “equipment” shall be substituted for the term “product”;
the term “Secretary” shall be substituted for “Commission” each place it appears (other than in section 6303(c) of title);
shall be applied, in the case of electric motors, as if the National Appliance Energy Conservation Act of 1987 was the Energy Policy Act of 1992;section 6297(a) of this title

shall be applied as if electric motors were fluorescent lamp ballasts and as if the National Appliance Energy Conservation Amendments of 1988 were the Energy Policy Act of 1992;section 6297(b)(1) of this title
shall be applied as if electric motors were fluorescent lamp ballasts and as if paragraph (5) of were ;section 6297(b)(4) of this titlesection 6295(g) of this titlesection 6313 of this title

notwithstanding any other provision of law, a regulation or other requirement adopted by a State or subdivision of a State contained in a State or local building code for new construction concerning the energy efficiency or energy use of an electric motor covered under this part is not superseded by the standards for such electric motor established or prescribed under if such regulation or requirement is identical to the standards established or prescribed under such section;section 6313(b) of this title
in the case of commercial clothes washers, shall be applied as if the National Appliance Energy Conservation Act of 1987 was the Energy Policy Act of 2005; andsection 6297(b)(1) of this title

shall apply with respect to the equipment described in beginning on the date on which a final rule establishing an energy conservation standard is issued by the Secretary, except that any State or local standard prescribed or enacted for the equipment before the date on which the final rule is issued shall not be preempted until the energy conservation standard established by the Secretary for the equipment takes effect.section 6297 of this titlesection 6311(1)(L) of this title

The provisions of , section 6296(a), (b), and (d) of this title, , and sections 6298 through 6306 of this title shall apply with respect to the equipment specified in subparagraphs (B), (C), (D), (I), (J), and (K) of to the same extent and in the same manner as they apply in part A. In applying such provisions for the purposes of such equipment, paragraphs (1), (2), (3), and (4) of subsection (a) shall apply.section 6295(p)(4) of this titlesection 6297(a) of this titlesection 6311(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: A standard prescribed or established under shall, beginning on the effective date of such standard, supersede any State or local regulation concerning the energy efficiency or energy use of a product for which a standard is prescribed or established pursuant to such section.section 6313(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the standard in the building code does not require that the energy efficiency of such product exceed the applicable minimum energy efficiency requirement in amended ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the standard in the building code does not take effect prior to the effective date of the applicable minimum energy efficiency requirement in amended ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1.

children.label: C
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), a standard prescribed or established under shall not supersede the standards established by the State of California set forth in Table C–6, California Code of Regulations, Title 24, Part 2, Chapter 2–53, for water-source heat pumps below 135,000 Btu per hour (cooling capacity) that become effective on .section 6313(a) of this titleJanuary 1, 1993

children.label: D
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), a standard prescribed or established under shall not supersede a State regulation which has been granted a waiver by the Secretary. The Secretary may grant a waiver pursuant to the terms, conditions, criteria, procedures, and other requirements specified in .section 6313(a) of this titlesection 6297(d) of this title

children.label: c
children.text: With respect to any electric motor to which standards are applicable under , the Secretary shall require manufacturers to certify, through an independent testing or certification program nationally recognized in the United States, that such motor meets the applicable standard.section 6313(b) of this title

Except as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), shall apply with respect to very large commercial package air conditioning and heating equipment to the same extent and in the same manner as applies under part A on .section 6297 of this titlesection 6297 of this titleAugust 8, 2005
Any State or local standard issued before , shall not be preempted until the standards established under take effect on .August 8, 2005January 1, 2010section 6313(a)(9) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Subsections (a), (b), and (d) of , subsections (m) through (s) of , and sections 6298 through 6306 of this title shall apply with respect to commercial refrigerators, freezers, and refrigerator-freezers to the same extent and in the same manner as those provisions apply under part A.section 6296 of this titlesection 6295 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: In applying those provisions to commercial refrigerators, freezers, and refrigerator-freezers, paragraphs (1), (2), (3), and (4) of subsection (a) shall apply.

children.label: A
children.text: shall apply to commercial refrigerators, freezers, and refrigerator-freezers for which standards are established under paragraphs (2) and (3) of to the same extent and in the same manner as those provisions apply under part A on , except that any State or local standard issued before , shall not be preempted until the standards established under paragraphs (2) and (3) of take effect.Section 6297 of this titlesection 6313(c) of this titlesection 6313(c) of this titleAugust 8, 2005August 8, 2005

children.label: B
children.text: In applying in accordance with subparagraph (A), paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a) shall apply.section 6297 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall apply to commercial refrigerators, freezers, and refrigerator-freezers for which standards are established under section 6313(c)(4) of this title to the same extent and in the same manner as the provisions apply under part A on the date of publication of the final rule by the Secretary, except that any State or local standard issued before the date of publication of the final rule by the Secretary shall not be preempted until the standards take effect.Section 6297 of this title2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: B
children.text: In applying in accordance with subparagraph (A), paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a) shall apply.section 6297 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: If the Secretary does not issue a final rule for a specific type of commercial refrigerator, freezer, or refrigerator-freezer within the time frame specified in section 6313(c)(5) of this title, subsections (b) and (c) of shall not apply to that specific type of refrigerator, freezer, or refrigerator-freezer for the period beginning on the date that is 2 years after the scheduled date for a final rule and ending on the date on which the Secretary publishes a final rule covering the specific type of refrigerator, freezer, or refrigerator-freezer.2section 6297 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Any State or local standard issued before the date of publication of the final rule shall not be preempted until the final rule takes effect.

children.label: A
children.text: In the case of any commercial refrigerator, freezer, or refrigerator-freezer to which standards are applicable under paragraphs (2) and (3) of , the Secretary shall require manufacturers to certify, through an independent, nationally recognized testing or certification program, that the commercial refrigerator, freezer, or refrigerator-freezer meets the applicable standard.section 6313(c) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, encourage the establishment of at least 2 independent testing and certification programs.

children.label: C
children.text: As part of certification, information on equipment energy use and interior volume shall be made available to the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), shall apply to automatic commercial ice makers for which standards have been established under to the same extent and in the same manner as the section applies under part A on .section 6297 of this titlesection 6313(d)(1) of this titleAugust 8, 2005

children.label: ii
children.text: Any State standard issued before , shall not be preempted until the standards established under take effect.August 8, 2005section 6313(d)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: In applying to the equipment under subparagraph (A), paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a) shall apply.section 6297 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), shall apply to automatic commercial ice makers for which standards have been established under to the same extent and in the same manner as the section applies under part A on the date of publication of the final rule by the Secretary.section 6297 of this titlesection 6313(d)(2) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Any State standard issued before the date of publication of the final rule by the Secretary shall not be preempted until the standards established under take effect.section 6313(d)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: In applying in accordance with subparagraph (A), paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a) shall apply.section 6297 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: If the Secretary does not issue a final rule for a specific type of automatic commercial ice maker within the time frame specified in , subsections (b) and (c) of shall no longer apply to the specific type of automatic commercial ice maker for the period beginning on the day after the scheduled date for a final rule and ending on the date on which the Secretary publishes a final rule covering the specific type of automatic commercial ice maker.section 6313(d) of this titlesection 6297 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Any State standard issued before the publication of the final rule shall not be preempted until the standards established in the final rule take effect.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary shall monitor whether manufacturers are reducing harvest rates below tested values for the purpose of bringing non-complying equipment into compliance.

children.label: B
children.text: If the Secretary finds that there has been a substantial amount of manipulation with respect to harvest rates under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall take steps to minimize the manipulation, such as requiring harvest rates to be within 5 percent of tested values.

children.label: A
children.text: If the Secretary does not issue a final rule for commercial clothes washers within the timeframe specified in , subsections (b) and (c) of shall not apply to commercial clothes washers for the period beginning on the day after the scheduled date for a final rule and ending on the date on which the Secretary publishes a final rule covering commercial clothes washers.section 6313(e)(2) of this titlesection 6297 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Any State or local standard issued before the date on which the Secretary publishes a final rule shall not be preempted until the standards established under take effect.section 6313(e)(2) of this title

The Secretary shall undertake an educational program to inform owners of laundromats, multifamily housing, and other sites where commercial clothes washers are located about the new standard, including impacts on washer purchase costs and options for recovering those costs through coin collection.

children.heading: Walk-in coolers and walk-in freezers.—
children.label: h Walk-in coolers and walk-in freezers.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, shall apply to walk-in coolers and walk-in freezers for which standards have been established under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of to the same extent and in the same manner as the section applies under part A on .section 6297 of this titlesection 6313(f) of this titleDecember 19, 2007

children.heading: .—State standards
children.label: ii .—State standards
children.text: Any State standard prescribed before , shall not be preempted until the standards established under paragraphs (1) and (2) of take effect.December 19, 2007section 6313(f) of this title

children.heading: .—Administration
children.label: B .—Administration
children.text: In applying to equipment under subparagraph (A), paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a) shall apply.section 6297 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: beginning on the day after the scheduled date for a final rule; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ending on the date on which the Secretary publishes a final rule covering the specific type of walk-in cooler or walk-in freezer.

children.heading: .—State standards
children.label: B .—State standards
children.text: Any State standard issued before the publication of the final rule shall not be preempted until the standards established in the final rule take effect.

children.heading: .—California
children.label: 3 .—California
children.text: Any standard issued in the State of California before , under title 20 of the California Code of Regulations, that refers to walk-in coolers and walk-in freezers, for which standards have been established under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of , shall not be preempted until the standards established under take effect.January 1, 2011section 6313(f) of this titlesection 6313(f)(4) of this title
